sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: FAM Dividend Focus Fund's Heico Corp(HEI.A) Holding History
feed.summary: This article details the FAM Dividend Focus Fund's investment history in Heico Corp (HEI.A). As of December 31, 2025, the fund holds 166,705 shares valued at $42.08 million, representing 6.1% of its portfolio. The article notes that the fund has made two purchases since Q4 2021 at an average price of $128.95, resulting in an estimated gain of $13.35 million or 62.09% on the holding.

feed.title: Heico Corp (HEI.A) Stock Up 3.9% and Still Undervalued -- GF Sco
feed.summary: Heico Corp (HEI.A) saw its stock rise by 3.9% to $209.02, despite a year-to-date decline, and is considered currently undervalued by GuruFocus' GF Value™ estimate. The company boasts a strong GF Score™ of 96/100, driven by excellent profitability and growth metrics, and recent insider buying activity signals confidence in its future. The stock's current P/E ratio is also below its historical median, further suggesting undervaluation.

feed.title: GrowGeneration Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release Conference Call for May 12, 2026
feed.summary: GrowGeneration Corp. announced it will release its first quarter 2026 financial results after market close on Monday, May 11, 2026. The company will host a conference call the following day, Tuesday, May 12, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. EDT to discuss the results and provide a business update. Investors can access the live webcast via the company's IR website or listen by phone.

feed.title: New York State Teachers Retirement System Trims Stake in PepsiCo, Inc. $PEP
feed.summary: The New York State Teachers Retirement System reduced its stake in PepsiCo by 2.7% in Q4, selling over 30,000 shares but still holding over 1 million shares. PepsiCo reported strong quarterly earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and announced a $10 billion share buyback program and a $1.4225 quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Hold" rating on the stock with an average target price of $170.26.

feed.title: US Bancorp DE Sells 3,879 Shares of Lockheed Martin Corporation $LMT

feed.title: Earnings Flash (CHD) Church & Dwight Co., Inc. Posts Q1 Adjusted EPS $0.95 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.93
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (CHD) announced Q1 adjusted earnings per share of $0.95, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $0.93. The company also reported Q1 revenue of $1.47 billion, which exceeded the FactSet estimate of $1.46 billion. This financial news highlights better-than-expected performance for the consumer products company in the first quarter.

feed.title: Piper Sandler Initiates Aflac Inc(AFL.US) With Buy Rating, Announces Target Price $130
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has initiated coverage of Aflac Inc (AFL.US) with a Buy rating. The investment bank also set a target price of $130 for the insurance company's stock. This initiation suggests a positive outlook on Aflac's future performance.

feed.title: Wells Fargo cuts Ryan Specialty stock rating on weaker growth outlook
feed.summary: Wells Fargo downgraded Ryan Specialty Group (NYSE:RYAN) to Equal Weight from Overweight, reducing its price target to $31.00 from $42.00 due to an anticipated weaker organic growth outlook. The firm noted that Ryan Specialty's new growth targets and margin compression fall below those of best-in-class retail brokers, leading Wells Fargo to believe competitors like AON and Arthur J. Gallagher will perform better. This downgrade follows Ryan Specialty's recent Q1 2026 results which surpassed expectations, but the company also adjusted its full-year guidance downwards, prompting similar price target cuts from Mizuho and Jefferies.

feed.title: GE Aerospace $GE Position Lessened by US Bancorp DE
feed.summary: US Bancorp DE reduced its stake in GE Aerospace by 1.6% in Q4, now holding 293,315 shares worth $90.35 million. Despite GE Aerospace beating Q1 EPS estimates and reporting a significant surge in orders, an internal outlook adjustment prompted some near-term selloff and increased volatility. Additionally, a senior vice president, Amy L. Gowder, executed a notable insider sale of 4,000 shares, decreasing her ownership by nearly 20%.

feed.title: US Bancorp DE Reduces Position in Comcast Corporation $CMCSA
feed.summary: US Bancorp DE has reduced its stake in Comcast Corporation by 18.8% in Q4, selling 384,146 shares, bringing their total holdings to 1,661,443 shares valued at approximately $49.66 million. Concurrently, CEO Michael J. Cavanagh also trimmed his position by 8.52% through the sale of 57,947 shares. Despite these reductions, Comcast reported strong Q4 earnings, beating analyst estimates with an EPS of $0.79 and revenue of $31.46 billion, while analysts generally maintain a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $34.94.

feed.title: Jennison Associates LLC Reduces Stake in AT&T Inc. $T

The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2775 per share, yielding 4.2%.

feed.title: DTE, Consumers rate hikes keep coming as outages drag on | Opinion
feed.summary: The author, a former Michigan resident, criticizes DTE Energy and Consumers Energy for persistent power outages and high rates, contrasting Michigan's poor performance with Illinois's reliable, cleaner energy grid. Michigan ranks last in the U.S. for outage length, with nearly half of DTE customers experiencing long outages in 2023, posing significant risks, especially for those dependent on medical equipment. The author suggests that Michigan could learn from Illinois's path to reform and improved service.

feed.title: US Bancorp DE Increases Stock Holdings in L3Harris Technologies Inc $LHX
feed.summary: US Bancorp DE has increased its stake in L3Harris Technologies Inc. by 1.3%, bringing its total holdings to 348,044 shares valued at $102.18 million. This comes after L3Harris reported strong Q1 results, beating EPS and revenue estimates, raising its FY2026 EPS outlook, and announcing a record backlog. Despite these positive indicators, the stock experienced a pullback due to investor concerns over guidance and valuation, alongside insider selling activity.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation Partnering with Feeding America Eastern Wisconsin and Fork Farms to Create Clock Tower Farms
feed.summary: Rockwell Automation is collaborating with Feeding America Eastern Wisconsin and Fork Farms to establish Clock Tower Farms, a hydroponic farming facility at Rockwell's Milwaukee headquarters. This initiative aims to combat hunger by growing fresh produce using automation technology, with an annual target of 200,000 pounds of leafy greens. The produce will be directly distributed to communities in Eastern Wisconsin, supporting healthier food access and addressing food insecurity.

feed.title: ICE (NYSE: ICE) Posts Record Q1 Revenue of $3B
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE) reported record net revenues of $3.0 billion for the first quarter of 2026, a 20% increase year-over-year, with GAAP diluted earnings per share jumping 80% to $2.48. The company's exchange segment contributed significantly, showing a 30% revenue growth. ICE also returned $848 million to stockholders through share repurchases and dividends while investing in strategic growth.

feed.title: Online hits 24% of consumer sales as Church & Dwight beats Q1 view
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) exceeded its first-quarter outlook, reporting a 0.2% increase in net sales and a 5.0% organic sales growth, driven by strong innovation and distribution. The company's global online sales now account for 24% of total consumer sales, and it reaffirmed its full-year outlook for organic sales growth of 3% to 4% and adjusted EPS growth of 5% to 8%. New product launches, particularly in THERABREATH™, ARM & HAMMER™ Cat Litter, HERO™, and TROJAN™, are expected to contribute significantly to organic growth.

feed.title: Moderna stock jumps after Q1 2026 beat (MRNA:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Moderna (MRNA) stock jumped approximately 9% in premarket trading after the biotech company reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 financials. The strong performance was primarily driven by higher COVID-19 vaccine sales in international markets, with revenue reaching $389M, representing about 260% year-over-year growth.

feed.title: Is Camden Property Trust (CPT) Pricing Reflect Recent REIT Sentiment Shift?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Camden Property Trust's (CPT) valuation through Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratios. The DCF model suggests CPT is 33.1% undervalued at $105 per share, indicating an intrinsic value of $157.07. However, its P/E ratio of 27.52x is above the industry average and a proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 17.88x, implying it might be overvalued based on earnings multiples.

feed.title: NetApp Google Cloud AI Push Highlights Valuation Gap And Growth Questions
feed.summary: NetApp announced new AI capabilities with Google Cloud, focusing on simplifying AI workload deployment for enterprises without re-architecting existing environments. This collaboration positions NetApp at the intersection of storage, data management, and enterprise IT for AI-heavy workloads. The market will be watching customer adoption and financial impact as NetApp's stock currently trades below analyst targets and its estimated fair value.

feed.title: Jennison Associates LLC Grows Stock Position in Citigroup Inc. $C

Other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc., Bank of New York Mellon Corp, and Capital World Investors also boosted their positions in Citigroup. The article also details recent positive, neutral, and negative news impacting Citigroup, including analyst target price increases and insider selling activity.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Cuts In-Flight Service On Hundreds Of Short Flights
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is removing in-flight services on approximately 450 daily flights under 349 miles starting May 19, 2026. While flights over 350 miles will now receive full beverage service, some popular short routes like San Francisco to Los Angeles will lose service entirely. First-class passengers remain unaffected, but economy and Delta Comfort passengers will experience these changes.

feed.title: Ford extends employee pricing to all customers for America at 250: You pay what we pay
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company has announced it is extending employee pricing to all customers on most new 2025 and 2026 Ford and Lincoln vehicles, celebrating America's 250th anniversary. This campaign, called "American Value. For American Values," is described as an investment in American manufacturing and communities, aiming to make vehicles more accessible. The offer runs through July 6, allowing customers to pre-qualify for financing online.

feed.title: Moderna beats revenue expectations despite wider loss
feed.summary: Moderna Inc. reported Q1 results exceeding revenue expectations, driven by higher international sales, despite a wider-than-expected loss due to a $0.9 billion litigation settlement charge. Shares rose 10.4% as investors focused on the revenue beat and the company's reaffirmed growth outlook of up to 10% revenue growth for 2026, excluding the one-time charge. The biotech firm also expects several new product approvals, including a seasonal flu vaccine.

feed.title: Moderna stocks: MRNA shares jump in US Stock market today after pharma giant surpasses Wall Street estimat
feed.summary: Moderna's shares surged over six percent after the company exceeded Wall Street's first-quarter revenue estimates, driven by strong international sales of its COVID-19 vaccine. Despite a quarterly net loss per share, the company reaffirmed its 2026 revenue growth forecast and is expanding beyond infectious diseases to include treatments for cancer and rare metabolic disorders.

feed.title: Duke Energy Carolinas Merger Puts Customer Savings And Growth Plans In Focus
feed.summary: The Public Service Commission of South Carolina has approved Duke Energy's plan to merge Duke Energy Carolinas and Duke Energy Progress into a single utility, aiming to deliver billions in guaranteed customer savings through streamlined operations. This consolidation supports Duke's broader grid and generation plans, addressing regional power demand and long-term load growth. Investors should monitor the integration process, realization of promised savings, and management of future capital needs, considering both the risks of integration and the benefits of more efficient planning.

feed.title: Results: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. Beat Earnings Expectations And Analysts Now Have New Forecasts
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:ZBH) surpassed earnings expectations with a statutory profit of US$1.22 per share, despite its stock dropping 11% post-results. Analysts have become more bullish on the company's earnings potential for 2026, updating EPS forecasts to US$4.94, a substantial gain from previous estimates, while revenue expectations remain in line with earlier projections. The consensus price target remains stable at US$98.52, suggesting that the improved earnings outlook has not yet translated into a long-term positive impact on the stock's valuation.

feed.title: Exxon: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. reported first-quarter earnings of $4.18 billion, or $1 per share, with adjusted earnings of $1.16 per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The oil and natural gas company also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $85.14 billion for the period. These results were generated by Automated Insights using data from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Deere names insider Brent Norwood as finance chief
feed.summary: Deere has appointed Brent Norwood, a company insider with over two decades of experience, as its new chief financial officer. Norwood, previously a vice president in the Construction and Forestry division and John Deere Power Systems, will assume the role on May 1st. He replaces Joshua Jepsen, who departed in February, and current interim finance chief Ryan Campbell.

feed.title: Goosehead Insurance (GSHD) insiders log net sale of 5,486 Class A shares
feed.summary: Insiders at Goosehead Insurance (GSHD), including the Mark & Robyn Jones Descendants Trust 2014 and individuals Mark Evan Jones and Robyn Mary Elizabeth Jones, converted and sold a net total of 5,486 Class A shares. These sales occurred on April 28–29, 2026, at weighted average prices between $48 and $49 per share, following conversions of LLC Units and Class B shares. Despite the sales, the reporting persons retain significant holdings through LLC Units and Class B shares, indicating continued economic exposure to the company.

feed.title: GH Research Raises $111 Million in Completed Share Offering to Fund Depression Pipeline
feed.summary: GH Research (GHRS) successfully completed an underwritten public offering, raising approximately $111.2 million by selling 6,527,779 ordinary shares at $18.00 each. The capital will be used to advance the company's depression therapies, including research, clinical development, and pre-commercialization efforts for treatment-resistant depression therapies. This equity raise reflects investor confidence in GH Research's pipeline despite its current lack of revenue and widening losses, partly offset by a strong balance sheet.

feed.title: Starbucks Corporation $SBUX Shares Purchased by US Bancorp DE
feed.summary: US Bancorp DE increased its stake in Starbucks by 6.1% in Q4, acquiring an additional 51,773 shares to hold a total of 894,155 shares valued at approximately $75.3 million. Starbucks recently reported strong Q2 earnings, beating analyst estimates and raising its FY26 guidance, driven by a successful "Back to Starbucks" turnaround initiative. Despite this operational momentum and a traffic rebound, higher labor costs and store investments are pressuring margins, leading to mixed analyst sentiment regarding the stock's valuation after its recent strong performance.
